game_name: Populous II: Trials of the Olympian Gods

special_mechanics.name: Divine Intervention Powers
special_mechanics.description: Player can cast 29 (30 in PC version) divine powers across six categories: earth, water, wind, fire, plants, and people. Powers deplete manna from a global meter that regenerates based on population size.

special_mechanics.name: Hero Transformation
special_mechanics.description: Each power category includes a hero form allowing player to transform leader into one of six legendary Greek mythology figures that wander the map attacking enemies.

special_mechanics.name: Deadly Fungus
special_mechanics.description: Plant category power creates fungus on ground that changes shape according to Conway's Game of Life rules and kills anyone stepping on it.

special_mechanics.name: Monster Spawning
special_mechanics.description: When either side controls approximately 75% of the map, indestructible ancient Greek monsters (e.g., Colossus, Medusa) spawn and wander indiscriminately, killing all in their path.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Gameplay documented but contains no quantitative physics parameters. This is a god game strategy title where player controls divine powers rather than direct character movement. Wikipedia source documents game mechanics and design philosophy but lacks technical movement/physics specifications.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Populous II: Trials of the Olympian Gods
